John Kirby, who has succeeded c. W. Van Ceave as
arch foe of all labor organizations which to be the
business of president of the National Association of Man
ufacurers sent an open letter to John Mitchell recently


In this letter Kirby rubs salt into the open wound of
labor relations by rehearsng story after story OF alleged
tlabor outrages How many of them real labor out
RACES and how many are ordinary crimes faxed to look
like evils resulting from strikes, say 1t is In
terestng however, that the week that Kirbys letter
published bomb throwing in war between protect
ed and unprotected gamblers in Chicago laid to the labor
people, and look for Mr. kirbys next pronuncia
mento to list this among union ne is cyl
IDENTIFY of those overweight souls to whom the ord
nary rules of evidence mean much or little according to
the way his mind is made up.


For all that union labor has been guilty Of ourages in
the past We should be glad to think that these things
over but the blind hate of men like Kirby and Van
Cleave forbds any confident hope. The Van Cleaves,
Krbys COMPRISES and Mtchells thrown into
by forces greater than they and greater than they
know, and made to fight like rats and dogs In pit Per
haps is much to blame another And at bottom
common citizens much to blame as they. For
make and unmake Institutions and institutions make
labor troubles.


Kirby declares that his union is seeking to protect
peaceful and law abiding citizens in their Godgiven
right to labor"


Does he know what that means! Does it mean only
that every has right to work strikebreaker
or otherwise for him and his associated employers?


Is it God given right to work for else only?


If that is all it amounts to- is it worth fighting for?


If the God given right to labor that God has
corded to most the sacred right to compete with each
other for jobs to be given not they please by other
men then all that is necessary for the reetsabsment of
slavery any country for the employers to organize
closely that they list name number, tag and rate all
the abores who may desire work. and give work refuse
it as they please.


If that is all there is to the God given right to labor God
has delegated to capital the absolute rership labor


Of course Kirby may the world is wide and labor
may find employment anywhere But it is not true. The
world is WIDE but it is owned Labor has ceased to own
any world.


If Kirby will throw himself into the fight for the strik
ing of the hand of monopoly from the ownership of the
earth he will show himself capable of looking at both
sides of the shield Labor and productive capital both
exploited bv monopoly.
